title: The Draft Is the Thinking
excerpt: I used to think writing starts after thinking. Now I think writing is the thinking.

title: Respect the Reader
excerpt: Readers do not owe me attention. If the first three lines are vague, the post is not ready.

title: Keep the Stack Small
excerpt: Each extra dependency is a future decision tax. Minimal tools force clearer architecture.

title: Thinking in First Principles
excerpt: Borrowing conclusions is fast, but brittle. Borrowing questions is better.

title: Compounding Habits
excerpt: The boring habit is often the compounding one. Read daily, write daily, ship weekly.

title: On Useful Ambition
excerpt: Ambition without direction becomes anxiety. Direction comes from choosing a problem and sticking with it.

title: Build In Public Carefully
excerpt: Publishing progress is useful. Publishing confusion is noise. The difference is whether the update helps someone else.

title: Notes Over Opinions
excerpt: Opinions age quickly. Notes age better. So I am writing notes from projects, books, and mistakes.

title: Write What You Can Defend
excerpt: Hot takes are easy. Defensible takes are harder. I want posts that survive a reread six months later.

title: Seventh Entry
excerpt: This blog engine is now behaving like a real publishing platform.
